competition_tldr: This competition focused on solving mathematical Olympiad problems under a strict 5-hour inference constraint using large language models ranging from 14B to 120B parameters. Winning approaches centered on optimizing inference infrastructure (vLLM, speculative decoding, quantization) and fine-tuning strategies (SFT, DPO, GRPO) to balance accuracy with output length. Teams further boosted scores through advanced voting mechanisms, dynamic resource allocation, and robust code execution pipelines to handle high-concurrency generation.
key_challenges: GPU KV cache exhaustion causing generation failures
Balancing model accuracy with output length constraints
Handling random guesses and uniform answer distributions
Internal execution timeouts and kernel crash recovery
Dynamic resource allocation for varying problem difficulty
all_models: Deepseek R1 distilled to Qwen 2.5 architecture
all_ensemble_patterns: Weighted voting system that prioritizes larger answer magnitudes, requiring candidates to meet minimum point thresholds and a margin over the next best answer to win.
Majority voting across up to four attempts per question, selecting the most frequent non-trivial result while ignoring 0/1 unless they are the only options.
Checkpoint merging (e.g., CoT * 0.3 + TIR * 0.7) and weighted voting algorithms that bonus identical boxed and code answers, alongside self-consistency early stopping.
all_post_processing: Regex post-processor to capture formatting edge cases like `\displaystyle` instead of `\boxed`.
Self-consistency voting, weighted voting that penalizes short code or small answer values, and early termination of generation based on answer convergence/divergence thresholds.
all_what_worked: Streaming inference with early termination to save GPU capacity
Prompting the model to verify answers for quick problems
Forcing a guess after a token limit to prevent empty outputs
API-ification via vLLM for benchmarking and remote GPU usage
Logging token counts and GPU KV cache to debug failure modes
Multi-stage fine-tuning (SFT + DPO/GRPO) successfully reduced output length while maintaining accuracy.
Model merging regained accuracy lost during GRPO training.
Speculative decoding (ReDrafter) and quantization (AWQ, FP8, 8-bit KV Cache) significantly boosted throughput.
Dynamic time buffering and difficulty prediction via ModernBERT improved resource allocation.
all_what_did_not_work: Termination based strictly on GPU KV cache usage (no longer relevant)
Magnitude-based vote manipulation (losing relevance as models stop guessing)
Applying a length penalty during GRPO training severely hurt accuracy on the 14B model, leading to its removal.
all_critical_findings: Small answers are likely random guesses when the AI gives up.
Answers are uniformly distributed (0-99999) rather than logarithmically distributed.
GPU KV cache hitting 100% causes generation failures.
Streaming/inference termination significantly improves throughput for sequential questions.
For the 14B model, applying a length penalty during GRPO training severely hurt accuracy, so it was removed.
Model merging was critical to regain accuracy lost during the GRPO process.
Focusing training data on harder, integer-answer problems from Olympiads and AoPS forums improved performance.
notable_individual_insights: Ideas behind my public submission scoring [14/50] and [21/50]: Small answers are likely random guesses when the AI gives up, and answers are uniformly distributed (0-99999) rather than logarithmically distributed.
Sharing of our methods (longest leaderboard award submission): Patching vLLM to maintain persistent, per-request Jupyter kernel states enables robust high-concurrency code execution.

Kaggle AIMO 2 Top Solutions Report (Generated by notebooklm): Applying a length penalty during GRPO training severely hurt accuracy on the 14B model, making model merging critical to regain lost performance.
Ideas behind my public submission scoring [14/50] and [21/50]: Streaming inference with early termination significantly improves throughput for sequential questions under strict GPU constraints.
Kaggle AIMO 2 Top Solutions Report (Generated by notebooklm): Focusing training data on harder, integer-answer problems from Olympiads and AoPS forums directly improved model performance.
